name: Hazel
description: The Hazel tree (Corylus avellana) holds a prominent place in Celtic mythology and folklore, revered as a symbol of wisdom, knowledge, and divination. It was believed to be a tree of great power, often associated with the Otherworld and possessing the ability to grant enlightenment. In Celtic lore, hazelnuts were said to fall into sacred pools or wells, where salmon would consume them, imbuing the fish with the tree's inherent wisdom. Consuming the salmon would then bestow this knowledge upon the individual. The Hazel was also linked to poetry and artistic inspiration. Its nuts were seen as a source of prophetic insight, and its branches were used to create wands for divination and dowsing. The presence of Hazel trees near sacred sites, such as wells and standing stones, further solidified its connection to the spiritual realm. The Hazel's wood was valued for its strength and flexibility, making it ideal for crafting tools and building materials. Furthermore, the Hazel tree symbolized protection, and its branches were sometimes used to ward off evil spirits or negative energies. The Hazel's overall association with wisdom, knowledge, and the Otherworld made it a central figure in Celtic beliefs and practices, embodying the pursuit of enlightenment and understanding.
subtitle: Wisdom, Knowledge, and the Otherworld

keyMyths.title: The Salmon of Knowledge
keyMyths.description: Hazelnuts fall into a sacred well, are eaten by salmon, and grant wisdom to whoever consumes the salmon. Fionn Mac Cumhaill gains knowledge by accidentally eating the Salmon of Knowledge.

keyMyths.title: Hazel as a Divining Rod
keyMyths.description: Hazel branches are used as dowsing rods to find water or other hidden resources. This reflects the tree's association with hidden knowledge and divination.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of Irish mythological figures and concepts.
